name: Basic Leftover Casserole:
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Leftover cooked chicken (or turkey, or beef)
Frozen pearl onions (minced shallots, sliced leeks)
2 cups milk OR 1 cup milk and 1/2 cup sharp cheese
Sherry, white wine, or vermouth (no more than 1/4 cup)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 9x9 baking dish, combine cooked pasta or rice, meat, and vegetables.
2. Prepare the sauce: melt butter in a medium saucepan over low heat. If sauteing onions, garlic, or other add-ins, use an additional tablespoon of butter.
3. Add flour to the melted butter, stirring until it forms a thick paste.
4. Gradually add milk (or milk and cheese), stirring until smooth.
5. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
6. Stir in grated cheese if making a cheese sauce, then stir until melted.
7. Season with salt, pepper, nutmeg, curry powder, sherry, white wine, or vermouth as desired.
8. Pour the sauce over the casserole mixture and gently stir to combine.
9. Transfer to preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) and bake, uncovered, for 30 minutes.
10. Allow to stand for about 10 minutes before serving.
This recipe is a versatile way to use leftovers, with varied flavor combinations each time you make it. It freezes well unless using a hard cheese that may separate or cause oil to float to the top; in such cases, add a breadcrumb topping.
